summary: In this brief post, Spolsky shares his frustration with learning ADO.NET, questioning whether it represents technological progress. He then addresses a more significant concern: developers treating his writings as scripture rather than thinking critically for themselves. He describes receiving emails from teams who quote his articles at each other during disagreements, expecting him to settle their disputes. He concludes that no methodology or external authority can substitute for independent thinking.
key_points: ADO.NET is presented as a possible regression rather than an improvement in data access technology
Developers are using Spolsky's writings as ammunition in team arguments rather than engaging in independent critical thinking
No external authority or methodology can compensate for a team's inability to think through their own problems
Context matters — generic advice from blog posts cannot be blindly applied to every team's unique situation
money_quotes: They are hitting each other over the head with various quotes from me, instead of thinking for themselves.
I haven't yet written the article that says that if you can't think for yourself, no amount of "methodology" is going to save you.
key_insight: No amount of expert opinion or methodology can substitute for a development team's ability to think critically about their own specific problems.
